channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Australian Open: Naomi Osaka stops playing because of an injury against Belinda Bencic
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Two-time Australian Open champion Naomi Osaka stopped playing because of a strained abdominal muscle after dropping the opening set of her third-round match against Belinda Bencic on Friday. “I kind of have a history of it, since I was a teenager.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Sabalenka is into the fourth round at the Australian Open, Naomi Osaka is out with injury
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Two-time defending Australian Open champion Aryna Sabalenka extended a few impressive streaks on Friday while becoming one of the first players to advance to the fourth round of the year's opening Grand Slam tournament.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Montrose boys basketball escapes a scare from Central as Red Hawk girls dominate Warriors
item.description: For the first time this season the high-flying Red Hawks of Montrose made the trip to the Grand Valley.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: CMU baseball, softball picked to top RMAC in 2025
item.description: In the RMAC preseason coaches poll, both CMU Baseball and CMU softball were selected to top the conference.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Alex Ovechkin breaks the record for most goalies scored on, beating Leevi Merilainen to reach 179
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — Alex Ovechkin broke the NHL record for the most goaltenders scored on, beating Leevi Merilainen in overtime to give the Washington Capitals a 1-0 victory over the Ottawa Senators on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A rare gold medal from the 1904 St. Louis Olympics is up for auction
item.description: A rare gold medal from the 1904 Olympics in St. Louis is being auctioned off as part of a larger collection of memorabilia from previous Games.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Boys Basketball: Rancho Buena Vista 63, La Costa Canyon 61
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI- Fantastic hoops game on Wednesday night with Rancho Buena Vista edging La Costa Canyon 63-61.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Gilgeous-Alexander scores 40 as Thunder roll past Cavaliers 134-114 in matchup of NBA leaders
item.description: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander had 40 points and eight assists in three quarters, and the Oklahoma City Thunder rolled past the Cleveland Cavaliers 134-114 in a matchup of teams with the best records in the NBA.
item.dc:creator: Cliff Brunt, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: San Diego Clippers Hometown Big Man
item.description: "It's honestly one of the more beautiful full circle moments I could've asked for." said the 6'11" Center Cisse.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Utah HC's youngest player having big impact
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Logan Cooley may only be 20 years old, but it is scary to think where the Utah Hockey Club would be without him this year. Cooley can't even drink legally, and he is second on the team in points with 12 goals and 25 assists.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Remembering 'Mr. Baseball' Bob Uecker
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- There are some people who just make you feel good about life. Bob Uecker was one of those individuals. He passed away Thursday at the age of 90 after a battle with lung cancer. Uecker did it all.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: J.T. Poston goes 10 under for a 1-shot lead among the usual low scores at The American Express
item.description: LA QUINTA, Calif. (AP) — J.T. Poston shot a 10-under 62 to take a one-stroke lead over Justin Lower in the opening round of The American Express on Thursday. Poston carded nine birdies and an eagle on the Nicklaus Tournament Course at PGA West in the Palm Springs-area desert.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: LSU football star Colin Hurley injured in on-campus car crash: police report
item.description: The cause of the wreck was not immediately made public; LSU officials did not provide additional comment since the freshman quarterback is a minor.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PGA Tour moves Genesis Invitational out of Riviera due to Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: The PGA Tour announced on Thursday it will move the Genesis Invitational to a different course due to the deadly Los Angeles wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utes earn first Big 12 road win, beating TCU 73-65
item.description: FORT WORTH, Tex. (AP) - After getting its first Big 12 Conference win on Saturday, the Runnin' Utes now have their first Big 12 winning streak. Gabe Madsen scored 17 points, Keanu Dawes added 16 and Utah never trailed in a 73-65 victory over TCU on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: White Sox announce 16 signings in 2025 international class
item.description: The Chicago White Sox have agreed to terms with 16 international players, including two ranked in the Top 100 available prospects, Alejandro Cruz and Orlando Patiño, and other prospects with potential.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Mike McCarthy won't return as the Cowboys' coach. Deion Sanders could be a candidate to replace him
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Mike McCarthy will not return as coach of the Dallas Cowboys, who are going on three decades since their last Super Bowl title, owner Jerry Jones said Monday. Jones said the organization and McCarthy mutually agreed to part ways.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI and SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cubs sign former Brewers starting pitcher to one-year deal
item.description: The Chicago Cubs have signed former Milwaukee Brewers starting pitcher Colin Rea to a new contract for the 2025 MLB season, while the Cubs have designated RHP Michael Arias for assignment to make room for Rea on the 40-man roster.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Patrick and Brittany Mahomes welcome third child
item.description: Brittany Mahomes gave birth to the couple’s third child, a girl named Golden Raye Mahomes, on Jan. 12, the couple announced on social media.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Texas man accused of sending Caitlin Clark violent messages on X: court docs
item.description: A Texas man is accused of sending sexually violent messages from his X account to Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark and then driving to Indianapolis, court documents reveal.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, Fox 5

item.title: New England Patriots hire former Super Bowl champ Mike Vrabel as next coach
item.description: Vrabel, a fan favorite during eight seasons as a player in New England where he was a member of its first three Super Bowl winners and an inductee into the team’s Hall of Fame in 2023, was the preferred candidate because of his long association with the franchise and coaching success.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jackson and Henry lead the way as the Ravens run over the rival Steelers 28-14 in the AFC playoffs
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Lamar Jackson handed off to Derrick Henry, then slid out to the left as if he still had the ball. A couple linebackers shifted into Jackson's path, leaving the middle open for Henry to run through for a 44-yard touchdown.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: BYU loses third straight, falling at TCU, 71-67
item.description: FORT WORTH, Tex. (ABC4 Sports) - The BYU basketball team has hit a funk at the beginning of the Big 12 Conference season. In a back-and-forth battle in Fort Worth, the Cougars fell 71-67 to TCU for its third straight loss.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Chicago Public Schools paid $390K to do-not-hire sports officials
item.description: Chicago Public Schools paid $390,000 to at least 36 people who were on a do-not-hire list to officiate sporting events.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Expect road closures in Santee for 2025 USATF National Race Walk Championships
item.description: The City of Santee is hosting the 2025 USATF National Race Walk Championships this weekend, and several road closures will be in place Sunday morning into the early afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: Bucs Bonus: Tampa Bay looking for similar results in Wild Card rematch against Washington
item.description: With the Buccaneers gearing up for their fifth straight Post Season run, Barber joins a special episode of Bucs Bonus to talk about what the team needs to do to contain rookie quarterback phenom Jayden Daniels and the Washington Commanders offense that has leveled up since the two teams met in week
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC
